mnemonics.title: Three Requirements of Critical Section
mnemonics.content: MUTUAL EXCLUSION: ONLY ONE AT A TIME. PROGRESS: WHO WANTS IN, LET THEM DECIDE. BOUNDED WAITING: NO STARVATION, LIMITED ENTRIES.

mnemonics.title: Process States for CS
mnemonics.content: ENTRY SECTION (ASK PERMISSION) -> CRITICAL SECTION (ACCESS SHARED) -> EXIT SECTION (RELEASE) -> REMAINDER SECTION (DO OTHER WORK)

mnemonics.title: Semaphore Operations
mnemonics.content: WAIT = DECREMENT AND IF NEGATIVE, SLEEP. SIGNAL = INCREMENT AND IF NON-POSITIVE, WAKE UP ONE.

mnemonics.title: Peterson's Algorithm Variables
mnemonics.content: FLAG[2] = WHO WANTS TO ENTER? TURN = WHOSE TURN IS IT? BOTH TRUE = TURN DECIDES.

acronyms.usage: The portion of code accessing shared resources that must be executed atomically by only one process at a time.
Semaphore operation that decrements the value and blocks if value becomes negative.
Semaphore operation that increments the value and wakes up a waiting process if any.
Hardware atomic instruction used for implementing mutual exclusion in critical sections.
Scheduling principle that can help achieve bounded waiting in critical section solutions.

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A GATE WITH ONE KEY. ONLY ONE PERSON CAN GO THROUGH AT A TIME. OTHERS MUST WAIT AT THE GATE.
DRAW A NUMBERED TICKET SYSTEM. AFTER GETTING A TICKET, A PERSON WILL BE CALLED WITHIN A FIXED NUMBER OF TURNS. NO INDEFINITE WAITING.
DRAW A NUMBERED COUNTER LIKE A PARKING LOT SIGN. AVAILABLE PARKING SPACES = SEMAPHORE VALUE. WHEN FULL (0), CARS WAIT.
DRAW TWO FLAGS (FLAG[0], FLAG[1]) AND ONE TURN ARROW. BOTH RAISE HANDS (FLAG=TRUE), THEN LOOK AT WHO'S TURN ARROW POINTS TO.
DRAW A SINGLE DOOR ROOM. ONLY ONE PERSON CAN ENTER AT A TIME. IF CONDITION NEEDED, WAIT IN ANTE-ROOM (QUEUE). SIGNAL OPENS DOOR FOR WAITING PERSON.

analogies.analogy: A SINGLE RESTROOM IN AN OFFICE. ONLY ONE PERSON CAN USE IT AT A TIME. OTHERS MUST WAIT OUTSIDE.
A CAR WITH ONE STEERING WHEEL. ONLY ONE DRIVER CAN CONTROL IT AT A TIME. MULTIPLE DRIVERS CAUSES CRASH.
A TOLL BOOTH WITH A LIMITED NUMBER OF TOKENS. EACH CAR TAKES ONE TOKEN TO PASS. IF NO TOKENS LEFT, CARS WAIT IN LINE.
A DOCTOR'S CLINIC WITH A NUMBERED QUEUE SYSTEM. AFTER GETTING A NUMBER, YOU KNOW YOU'LL BE CALLED WITHIN THE NEXT N PATIENTS.

analogies.analization: AN EMPTY MEETING ROOM. IF NO ONE IS INSIDE AND PEOPLE WANT TO ENTER, THOSE OUTSIDE SHOULD DECIDE WHO GOES IN NEXT WITHOUT UNNECESSARY DELAYS.
